description:
Implement Phase 2 of the WiFi Heatmap PRD: LiDAR/ARKit room scanning to generate the 2D floor plan, followed by standard survey mode with AR position tracking. See docs/Heatmap-Phase-2-AR-Map.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AR camera feed shows surface detection overlay
- LiDAR/non-LiDAR device generated floor plan shows walls accurately
- Generated floor plan has correct real-world dimensions
- AR position tracking during survey places measurement dots correctly
- Survey workflow after map generation identical to Phase 1
title: Heatmap Phase 2: AR-Assisted Map

description:
Implement Phase 1 of the WiFi Heatmap PRD: Core models, measurement engines, and the Blueprint image import + tap-to-measure UI for macOS and iOS. See docs/Heatmap-Phase-1-Blueprint.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can import a PNG/JPEG/PDF floor plan and see it rendered on the canvas
- Two-point calibration sets correct pixels-per-meter ratio
- Tapping/clicking the canvas creates a MeasurementPoint with valid RSSI
- Heatmap overlay updates within 500ms of adding a new point
- Project save/load round-trips all data without loss
- Live RSSI updates at 1Hz during active survey
title: Heatmap Phase 1: Blueprint Survey
close_reason: Phase 1 Blueprint Walk Survey implemented - floor plan import, click/tap measurement, IDW heatmap rendering complete

description: Build HeatmapSurveyModels, WiFiMeasurementEngine, HeatmapRenderer in NetMonitorCore. Write unit tests for IDW interpolation and data model serialization. (Phase 1, Step 1)

Commit b7611e7 fixed ARPScannerService: (1) timeout now returns false (was true, causing phantom devices), (2) supports all active interfaces not just en0/en1 (fixes hotspot /28 subnets). Existing tests (NetworkDetailViewScanRegressionTests) inject ARPScannerService but don't test its internal behavior. Need: (1) test that timeout returns false, (2) test that multiple interfaces are scanned (mockable via network interface enumeration), (3) test that hotspot subnets are handled correctly. File: Tests/NetMonitor-macOSTests/ARPScannerServiceTests.swift
title: Test gap: ARPScannerService multi-interface + timeout fix not covered

description: Zero UI test coverage for the new per-network dashboard. Cards have accessibility IDs: network_detail_row_activity, network_detail_row_health, network_detail_card_isp, network_detail_card_latency, network_detail_card_connectivity, network_detail_card_intel, network_detail_panel_devices. NetworkDevicesPanel has: dashboard_activity_rangePicker, scan button, sort menu, search field. Test: dashboard cards visible when network selected, range picker 24H/7D/30D, scan button exists/tappable, device search field exists, sort menu has expected options. Create NetworkDetailViewUITests.swift in Tests/NetMonitor-macOSUITests/ using XCTest. ~8 tests.
title: NetworkDetailView dashboard UI tests

description: NetworkDetailView has a gatewayLatencyHistory computed property with 3-step fallback chain: 1) gateway-named target in recentLatencies, 2) first ICMP target with data, 3) any target with data. Test via MonitoringSession state. Also test .onAppear auto-start monitoring behavior and .onChange profile update propagation via MonitoringSession and NetworkProfileManager. Create NetworkDetailViewTests.swift in Tests/NetMonitor-macOSTests/. ~8 tests.
title: NetworkDetailView gateway latency fallback + lifecycle tests
MonitoringSession core loop extended tests
description: MonitoringSession has only 4 tests (no targets, stop idempotency, pruning). Core loop untested: startMonitoring with enabled targets sets isMonitoring=true, measurement persistence, latency buffer cap at 20 entries FIFO, onlineTargetCount/offlineTargetCount/averageLatencyString derived properties, stopMonitoring cancels tasks and updates SessionRecord, latestMeasurement(for:) lookup, network error marks target unreachable. Read NetMonitor-macOS/Platform/MonitoringSession.swift first to understand MonitorServiceProviding. Create MonitoringSessionExtendedTests.swift. ~12 tests.

title: NetworkHealthScoreMacViewModel unit tests
description: NetworkHealthScoreMacViewModel in NetworkHealthScoreView.swift has zero test coverage. Depends on PingServiceProtocol which is mockable. Need MockPingService returning controlled AsyncStream<PingResult>. Test: isCalculating transitions, score populated after successful ping, handles empty stream, correct packetLoss ratio from mixed timeout/ok results, latencyMs is average of non-timeout results. Create NetworkHealthScoreViewModelTests.swift in Tests/NetMonitor-macOSTests/. ~8 tests.

Zero coverage for two testable pure types in the macOS dashboard. LatencyStats has avg/min/max/jitter/histogramBuckets/normalizedHeights with no tests. NetworkHealthScoreService.computeScore() and .grade() have no tests despite complex threshold logic. Create DashboardModelsTests.swift and NetworkHealthScoreServiceTests.swift in Tests/NetMonitor-macOSTests/. ~25 tests using Swift Testing with parameterized arguments at every threshold boundary.
title: DashboardModels + NetworkHealthScoreService unit tests

description:
Implement Phase 3 of the WiFi Heatmap PRD: Simultaneous LiDAR scanning and Wi-Fi measurement at 2Hz. Real-time Metal rendering of map and heatmap, plus post-scan IDW refinement. See docs/Heatmap-Phase-3-AR-Continuous.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 2D map renders new areas within 200ms
- Heatmap colors appear on map within 1 sec
- Pause/resume preserves data and tracking
- Post-scan IDW refinement completes successfully
title: Heatmap Phase 3: AR Continuous Scan

description: Implement Phase 3 of the WiFi Heatmap PRD: Simultaneous LiDAR scanning and Wi-Fi measurement at 2Hz. Real-time Metal rendering of map and heatmap, plus post-scan IDW refinement. See docs/Heatmap-Phase-3-AR-Continuous.md.
title: Heatmap Phase 3: AR Continuous Scan

title: Metal Incremental Map Renderer

Implement the layout with top AR camera feed and bottom 2D map. Add pause/resume controls and post-scan completion transitions.

description: Implement PDF export of the heatmap view with legend and summary statistics table. (Phase 1 P1)
title: WiFi Heatmap macOS PDF Export
WiFi Heatmap Phase 3 — AR Continuous Scan
description: Phase 3 implements real-time AR continuous scanning for iOS (LiDAR required). Three concurrent pipelines run simultaneously: AR mesh capture (60fps), Wi-Fi measurement (2Hz adaptive), and Metal-based map rendering (10Hz). Users walk through a space while the app builds a real-time 2D map with heatmap overlay using persistent Metal textures (2048x2048). WiFiman color scheme (blue→cyan→green→yellow→orange→red). Split-screen UI shows AR camera (top 40%) and growing 2D map (bottom 60%). Includes pause/resume with AR relocalization, finish scan with IDW refinement, post-scan visualization switching, and comprehensive performance management (CPU<60%, GPU<40%, memory<600MB, battery<15%/30min). Thermal throttling via ThermalThrottleMonitor integration. P1 features: AP roaming overlay, walking path trace, coverage completeness indicator.

Phase 2 adds AR-assisted map creation and survey for iOS. Users scan their room with the device camera (LiDAR preferred, non-LiDAR supported with reduced accuracy). ARKit mesh data is processed through a pipeline (height filter → XZ projection → rasterization → Gaussian blur → contour detection) to generate a floor plan automatically. After scanning, the app transitions to Phase 1 survey mode with the AR-generated floor plan pre-loaded and pre-calibrated. AR position tracking enables auto-placement of measurement points. Includes tracking loss fallback to manual tap mode, floor plan editing (P1), and mesh classification for doors/windows (P1). iOS only — requires ARKit support.
title: WiFi Heatmap Phase 2 — AR Map Creation

description: Phase 1 implements the core blueprint-based Wi-Fi heatmap survey for both macOS and iOS. Users import a floor plan image, optionally calibrate scale with a two-point measurement, then walk the space tapping/clicking to collect Wi-Fi measurements at each location. The app renders IDW-interpolated heatmap overlays showing signal strength, SNR, speed, and latency. Includes project save/load (.netmonsurvey format), PDF export (macOS), measurement management (inspect, delete, undo), and live RSSI monitoring. macOS: full 5-visualization-type experience with CoreWLAN data. iOS: 3 visualization types with liquid glass styling and NEHotspotNetwork data.
title: WiFi Heatmap Phase 1 — Blueprint Walk Survey

description: Existing ISPCardErrorSurfacingTests.swift only tests error paths. Create ISPCardViewModelExtendedTests.swift in Tests/NetMonitor-macOSTests/. Reuse FailingISPService/SucceedingISPService stubs. ~10 tests.
title: ISPCardViewModel + ConnectivityCardViewModel extended tests
